meta.keywords: 4 days Siem Reap Phnom Kulen tour Malaysia, Angkor Wat Kulen Mountain itinerary, River of 1000 Lingas Cambodia, Cambodia unique tour package, Dhesu Cambodia

hero.title: 4 Days: Angkor Kingdom
hero.intro: Two of Cambodia's greatest sites in one four-day package. The Angkor Archaeological Park — Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, Ta Prohm, and the Pre Rup sunset — on Day 2. Then Phnom Kulen National Park on Day 3: the sacred mountain where the Khmer Empire was born in AD 802, where the River of a Thousand Lingas flows with carvings of Shiva and Vishnu, and where waterfalls drop through jungle you can swim in. Most Cambodia visitors never make it here.
hero.heroImageAlt: Phnom Kulen National Park waterfalls sacred mountain Cambodia Khmer Empire birthplace

includes: Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, L = Lunch, D = Dinner)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary
excludes: Travel insurance (ETIQA Area 2 Gold from RM41)

overview.heading: 4 Days Cambodia: The Full Picture
body: This package is for travellers who want to go beyond the standard Angkor circuit. Most Cambodia tours begin and end in Siem Reap with the temples. This one adds the experience that most visitors never discover: Phnom Kulen — the sacred mountain where the Khmer Empire was founded in AD 802, and one of the most remarkable natural and historical sites in Southeast Asia.

Day 2 covers the full Angkor Archaeological Park circuit: Angkor Wat at sunrise, the walled city of Angkor Thom with its 200-faced Bayon, Ta Prohm with its tree roots splitting ancient stone, and Pre Rup for the classic sunset. Everything the temples have to offer, unrushed.

Day 3 takes you north of Siem Reap to Phnom Kulen National Park. This is the mountain where King Jayavarman II declared independence from the Javanese kingdom and established the Khmer Empire. The River of a Thousand Lingas flows through the park — a stretch of river where the sandstone bed is carved with over 1,000 lingams (representations of Shiva) and depictions of Vishnu and Brahma, submerged beneath shallow rushing water. A reclining Buddha carved from a single sandstone boulder sits at the mountain summit. Two waterfalls drop into swimming holes in the jungle. And almost no foreign tourists ever come here.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Cambodia Package Stands Apart

items.title: Phnom Kulen — Birthplace of the Khmer Empire
items.body: In AD 802, King Jayavarman II climbed this mountain and performed a ceremony that declared the Khmer kingdom's independence and established himself as the first universal monarch — the event that historians mark as the birth of the Khmer Empire that built Angkor. Phnom Kulen (Mountain of the Lychees) is now a national park revered as the most sacred mountain in Cambodia. Most visitors to Siem Reap never make it here.
items.imageAlt: Phnom Kulen National Park sacred mountain Cambodia Khmer Empire

items.title: River of a Thousand Lingas — Carvings Beneath the Water
items.body: The Kbal Spean river flows over a sandstone riverbed that has been carved with more than 1,000 lingams (sacred representations of Shiva) along with depictions of Vishnu reclining on the cosmic serpent, Brahma, and various mythological figures. The carvings lie beneath a few centimetres of clear rushing water, catching the light in a scene that feels genuinely sacred. These were carved in the 11th century to purify the water flowing down to fertilise the Angkor rice plains below.
items.imageAlt: River of Thousand Lingas Kbal Spean Cambodia sandstone carvings underwater

items.title: Angkor Wat & The Full Temple Circuit
items.body: The world's largest religious monument covered properly in a full unrushed day. Angkor Wat at sunrise, Angkor Thom's Bayon with 200 stone faces, the Baphuon pyramid temple, the Terrace of Elephants, Ta Prohm with its famous tree roots, Banteay Kdei, and Pre Rup at sunset. Your licensed guide explains what you're seeing and why it was built.
items.imageAlt: Angkor Wat temple sunrise reflection Cambodia

items.title: Banteay Srei — The Women's Temple
items.body: One of Angkor's finest temples and one most tour groups skip. Banteay Srei was built in the 10th century in pink sandstone and is covered in the most intricate bas-relief carving in the entire Angkor complex — so detailed and fine that it was attributed to women (hence the name). The carvings of celestial dancers, mythological scenes, and decorative panels are remarkably well-preserved. Nearby Preah Dak village demonstrates traditional palm sugar production.
items.imageAlt: Banteay Srei pink sandstone temple intricate carvings Angkor Cambodia

days.title: Arrival Siem Reap — City Tour & Pub Street
days.description: Arrive at Siem Reap International Airport and transfer to hotel. Afternoon city orientation tour: Wat Preah Prom Rath (the most beautiful temple in Siem Reap city), the twin Preah Ang Chek and Preah Ang Chorm Shrines (the Sisters Temple), Royal Gardens, and Artisans Angkor crafts centre — where traditional Khmer silk weaving and craftsmanship are demonstrated. Dinner included. After dinner, leisurely walk around Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Siem Reap airport arrival & hotel transfer
Preah Ang Chek & Preah Ang Chorm Sisters Temple

days.title: Full Day Angkor Archaeological Circuit | Pub Street
days.description: Full day at the Angkor Archaeological Park. Morning: Angkor Wat at sunrise — the 350-metre causeway approach, the outer gallery bas-reliefs, and the five central towers. Angkor Thom via the South Gate; the Bayon temple with its 200 carved stone faces on 54 towers; Baphuon pyramid temple; Terrace of Elephants and Terrace of the Leper King. Afternoon: Ta Prohm (the Tomb Raider temple with its tree roots splitting ancient walls), Banteay Kdei, Srah Srang royal bathing reservoir, and Pre Rup sunset pyramid. Dinner included. Evening at Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Terrace of Elephants & Terrace of Leper King

days.title: Phnom Kulen National Park — Birthplace of the Khmer Empire
days.description: Full day at Phnom Kulen National Park — the sacred mountain 48 kilometres north of Siem Reap where the Khmer Empire was born in AD 802. Morning: walk the sacred jungle trails to Kbal Spean, the River of a Thousand Lingas, where over 1,000 lingams and mythological figures are carved into the submerged sandstone riverbed. Ascend to the summit to see the Preah Ang Thom, a colossal reclining Buddha carved from a single sandstone boulder. Swim in the mountain waterfalls descending through jungle. Afternoon: visit to Banteay Srei (the 10th-century pink sandstone temple with the finest carvings in the Angkor complex) and the nearby village of Preah Dak where traditional palm sugar production is demonstrated. Dinner and overnight in Siem Reap.
suggestedActivities: Kbal Spean River of a Thousand Lingas
Reclining Buddha summit of Kulen Mountain

days.title: Morning at Leisure — Depart Siem Reap
days.description: Morning at leisure for personal activities or last-minute souvenir shopping. Siem Reap's Old Market (Psar Chas) is the best source for silk scarves, silver jewellery, handmade crafts, and Cambodian spices. Transfer to Siem Reap International Airport for onward journey.

whyBook.heading: Cambodia Made Easy — By the Agency That Has Done It Since 1988
whyBook.body: Phnom Kulen is not on most Cambodia itineraries because the logistics are difficult: the park has limited entry numbers, the road is rough, and the sites require a knowledgeable guide to find and understand. Dhesu has been doing this since 1988. Our guides know the park, speak with the rangers, and ensure you spend your day on the mountain rather than navigating it.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We handle Cambodia visa documentation guidance, Angkor and Kulen pass arrangements, and all ground logistics.

points.title: 35+ Years of Cambodia Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Cambodia. Our guides know both the temples and the less-visited sites that make this country extraordinary.

points.title: Daily Departures, No Waiting
points.body: Your trip runs on your chosen dates with your own private guide and vehicle. No group minimums. No sharing with strangers.

points.title: Muslim-Friendly Options
points.body: Halal meals arranged at no extra cost. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap included on request. All dietary requirements accommodated.

sections.heading: 4 Day Siem Reap & Phnom Kulen Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
This package is for travellers who want to understand Cambodia beyond the famous monuments. Phnom Kulen is the most sacred mountain in Cambodia and the site where the Khmer Empire was born — yet it appears on very few tour itineraries because it requires a full day's commitment, an early start, and a guide who knows the park.

The combination of the Angkor temple circuit on Day 2 and Phnom Kulen on Day 3 gives you two completely different Cambodias: the stone-carved monuments of a medieval empire at the height of its power, and the sacred natural landscape where that empire began. Together they are more meaningful than either experience alone.

sections.heading: Phnom Kulen National Park: What to Know Before You Go
sections.body:
Phnom Kulen is a 48-kilometre drive from Siem Reap, taking approximately 1.5 hours on roads that become rougher near the park entrance. The park is open from 8am and has a daily visitor limit — early arrival is essential, particularly during peak season. The main sites are accessible on foot via jungle trails.

The River of a Thousand Lingas (Kbal Spean) requires a 30-minute walk each way through dense jungle. Comfortable shoes with good grip are essential. The waterfalls at Phnom Kulen are fed by seasonal rainfall and are most impressive between August and November. The reclining Buddha at the summit is accessible by a wide stepped path and requires about 20 minutes from the car park. Dress modestly — this is an active sacred site.

sections.heading: Banteay Srei: The Temple Most Tour Groups Skip
sections.body: Banteay Srei lies 25 kilometres northeast of Siem Reap in the same direction as Phnom Kulen, making it a natural addition to the Day 3 itinerary. Built in 967 AD in pink sandstone and dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva, Banteay Srei is smaller than the main Angkor temples but contains some of the finest stone carving anywhere in the Angkor complex. The intricate decorative panels, ceiling carvings, and pediments are far more detailed than anything at Angkor Wat itself. The name means 'Citadel of Women' and refers to the belief that only women with supernatural skill could have created such delicate work.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Cambodia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. Options include the Cambodia e-Visa (apply online before departure, approximately USD 36, processed in 3 business days) or visa on arrival at Siem Reap airport (approximately USD 30, cash only). Dhesu provides pre-departure guidance on the visa process.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Phnom Kulen National Park?
items.answer: Phnom Kulen is accessible year-round but the waterfalls are most impressive from August to November when rainfall has been heaviest. The River of a Thousand Lingas is clearest from December to February when water levels are lower and the carvings more visible. November to February offers the most comfortable temperatures for the jungle walk to Kbal Spean.

items.question: How difficult is the Kbal Spean walk?
items.answer: The walk to the River of a Thousand Lingas at Kbal Spean is approximately 1.5 kilometres each way on a jungle trail with some uneven terrain and tree roots. It is accessible for most reasonably fit adults. Allow 30-45 minutes each way. Comfortable shoes with grip are essential — sandals are not suitable. The trail is shaded by jungle canopy throughout.

items.question: Is the park entrance included in the package price?
items.answer: Yes. The Phnom Kulen National Park entrance fee and the Angkor Archaeological Park entrance pass are both included in the package price. No additional entrance payments are required.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: This is a 100% private tour. You have your own licensed English-speaking guide and private vehicle for all 4 days. The itinerary follows your own pace. Dhesu does not run group departures for Cambodia.

items.question: Can I arrange halal food throughout the tour?
items.answer: Yes. Halal meals can be arranged throughout your stay at no extra cost. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap can be included in your itinerary on request.
